definition: The ECB accumulated ~€750B in Italian sovereign debt (~25% of outstanding BTPs). PEPP reinvestment flexibility was the de facto spread stabilizer; its wind-down removes this marginal buyer. TPI is untested with conditionality requirements that may make it unavailable when needed. The equilibrium BTP-Bund spread without ECB flow support is likely wider than current ~130-150bp.
evidence: ECB holds ~€750B in BTPs (~25% of outstanding)
PEPP reinvestment flexibility wind-down removes marginal buyer
OMT precedent (never activated, sufficient by existence) could apply to TPI
open_questions: Will TPI's mere existence be sufficient to contain spreads (OMT analogy)?
What is the equilibrium BTP-Bund spread without ECB flow support?
